text: In general single-cell RNA-seq quality control, cells are typically retained if they express at least 200 genes, and a mitochondrial content threshold between 5 and 20 percent is applied depending on tissue. Principal component analysis commonly retains between 10 and 50 components, and Leiden clustering resolution is usually chosen between 0.4 and 1.2 to balance over- and under-clustering.
title: Current best practices in single-cell RNA-seq analysis

text: For peripheral blood mononuclear cells, a mitochondrial cutoff around 5 percent is appropriate because circulating immune cells have low mitochondrial content. Around 10 to 30 principal components capture the major immune populations, and a Leiden resolution near 0.5 resolves the main T cell, B cell, NK cell, and monocyte populations without fragmenting them.
title: Massively parallel digital transcriptional profiling of single cells

text: In bronchoalveolar lavage samples from respiratory disease, a higher mitochondrial threshold of up to 10 percent is often used because airway and immune cells in inflamed tissue show elevated mitochondrial content, and overly stringent thresholds discard viable cells. Harmony integration is recommended when combining multiple patients.
title: Single-cell landscape of bronchoalveolar immune cells in COVID-19

text: Harmony provides fast and accurate integration of single-cell datasets across batches and patients by correcting principal component embeddings, so that downstream clustering reflects cell type rather than sample of origin. It is the recommended integration method for multi-patient studies with moderate batch effects.
title: Fast, sensitive and accurate integration of single-cell data with Harmony

text: The Leiden algorithm improves on Louvain by guaranteeing well-connected communities. Clustering resolution controls granularity, with higher values yielding more clusters. Values between 0.4 and 1.0 are typical for immune tissues, while denser tissues may require higher resolution.
title: From Louvain to Leiden: guaranteeing well-connected communities

text: A common workflow normalises counts to 10000 per cell followed by log transformation, selects approximately 2000 highly variable genes, and computes principal components on the scaled data before neighbour graph construction and clustering.
title: SCANPY: large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis

text: In solid tumour samples, mitochondrial thresholds are often relaxed to 15 to 20 percent because malignant and stressed cells display high mitochondrial activity. A higher Leiden resolution of 0.8 to 1.2 helps separate closely related malignant and stromal subpopulations.
title: Single-cell analysis of tumour microenvironment

text: Default clustering resolution values such as 0.8 frequently cause over-clustering, producing spurious clusters that do not correspond to biological populations. Tissue-matched resolution selection reduces this artifact substantially.
title: Benchmarking single-cell clustering parameters
